key_insight: A thermodynamic, multiscale treatment of hydrogen trapping around bcc iron screw dislocations shows that a few energetically relevant core/site configurations dominate the interaction landscape.
engineering_relevance: The results provide a simplified but physically grounded basis for predicting hydrogen embrittlement and incorporating hydrogen-dislocation interactions into plasticity models.
method: Molecular simulations with a neural-network interatomic potential for bcc Fe–H.
Systematic exploration of easy-core and hard-core screw dislocation structures, trapping site types, and hydrogen concentrations.
Thermodynamic weighting of binding energies plus assessment of an elastic dipole approximation.
evidence_signal: The framework is benchmarked against the limited available density functional theory data and used to rationalize experimental hydrogen solubility limits.
watch_item: The simplified elastic dipole description is only valid within a limited regime, so core structure and site-specific trapping must be checked before using it in larger-scale models.
paper_title: Hydrogen segregation around a straight screw dislocation in bcc iron
